item.title: House brawl, road rehab, cruise violence
item.ns0:title: House brawl, road rehab, cruise violence
item.description:
Tonight on Our News, tempers flare in the House of Assembly for a second straight day as a heated debate over campaign contributions leads to a direct confrontation between the House Speaker and the Long Island MP. We break down the government's massive $98 million investment into national road rehabilitation and examine the Tourism Minister’s stern response to a violent brawl involving cruise passengers. Our team also investigates a tragic snorkeling incident off West Bay Street and follows the Coalition of Independents as they take their 2026 general election grievances to the courts. Catch the full newscast for these stories, a major airlift update, and your complete tropical weather outlook.
-----
Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns1:encoded:
Tonight on Our News, tempers flare in the House of Assembly for a second straight day as a heated debate over campaign contributions leads to a direct confrontation between the House Speaker and the Long Island MP. We break down the government's massive $98 million investment into national road rehabilitation and examine the Tourism Minister’s stern response to a violent brawl involving cruise passengers. Our team also investigates a tragic snorkeling incident off West Bay Street and follows the Coalition of Independents as they take their 2026 general election grievances to the courts. Catch the full newscast for these stories, a major airlift update, and your complete tropical weather outlook.
-----

item.ns0:summary:
Tonight on Our News, tempers flare in the House of Assembly for a second straight day as a heated debate over campaign contributions leads to a direct confrontation between the House Speaker and the Long Island MP. We break down the government's massive $98 million investment into national road rehabilitation and examine the Tourism Minister’s stern response to a violent brawl involving cruise passengers. Our team also investigates a tragic snorkeling incident off West Bay Street and follows the Coalition of Independents as they take their 2026 general election grievances to the courts. Catch the full newscast for these stories, a major airlift update, and your complete tropical weather outlook.

item.ns0:author: Our News Bahamas
